paper_title: Modeling Sea Level and Land Uplift in the Santa Elena Peninsula

methods.description: This method models the coast of the Santa Elena Peninsula using bathymetric readings from the Instituto Oceanográfico de la Armada (INOCAR) and the Instituto Geográfico Militar Ecuatoriano. It aims to understand the historical changes in sea level and land uplift.

steps.description: Model the coast based on the bathymetric readings from the Instituto Oceanográfico de la Armada (INOCAR 1980).
outputs: coastal model based on INOCAR data

steps.description: Model the coast based on the modern sea level isolines created from bathymetric readings from the Instituto Geográfico Militar Ecuatoriano.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch sea level isolines from Instituto Geográfico Militar
outputs: coastal model based on modern sea level isolines

methods.description: This method reproduces the coastal model by Stothert and colleagues for the Santa Elena Peninsula using Geographic Information Systems (GIS) to visualize coastal geomorphology and archaeological site situations over time.

steps.description: Reproduce the model by Stothert and colleagues for the Santa Elena Peninsula coast using Geographic Information Systems.
steps.searchable_summary: use GIS to reproduce Stothert's coastal model

methods.description: This method uses a TIN model of isobaths to perform a viewshed analysis, determining which land and water features are visible from Las Vegas archaeological sites.

steps.description: Use the TIN model of the isobaths produced by the Instituto Geográfico Militar for viewshed analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform viewshed analysis using TIN model
